reasoning: FIDE officially publishes their rating lists only once per month, on the 1st of the month. Therefore, the Elo ratings reflected on the Wikipedia 'FIDE rankings' page typically remain completely static from the start of the month until the next month's update. A mid-month update (before March 8 or March 31) that increases Tan Zhongyi's rating by 1% (more than 25 Elo points) compared to March 1 is practically impossible because no new official lists are published between the 1st and the end of the month. Even in the highly unlikely event that the April 2026 FIDE rating list is released early and processed by Wikipedia editors on March 31, Tan Zhongyi gaining 25+ points in a single month is extremely rare, given standard Elo k-factors and the fact that the Candidates tournament begins in late March (and would not be rated until May). Probabilities are kept at the minimum boundary prescribed by the prompt (0.05 for near-term, 0.06 for long-term).
